title: REPORTS-DEPTH
scope: Phase 27 [REPORTING + BI] shipped a lean security-hardened reports MVP: ReportBuilderService SAFE SQL allowlist (ALLOWED_TABLES payments/invoices/leases + ALLOWED_FIELDS map + ALLOWED_OPERATORS + mandatory landlord_id WHERE + parameterised Eloquent only) + saved_reports + Builder.vue form-builder UI + scheduled_reports + ScheduledReportDelivery i18n mailable + SendScheduledReports artisan + dailyAt 06:00 cron + CohortService/NoiService/ForecastService (golden-query analytics, not custom-builder). Scout confirmed: no parent_report_id link (no drill-down hierarchy); no report_templates table (no marketplace); no report_metrics table (no custom composite KPIs like occupancy*avg_rent); scheduled-report builder has no preview (operator hits save blind, waits for cron tick); Phase 36 [INSIGHT] Dashboard.vue widgets are ad-hoc PHP arrays not driven by a landlord_dashboards table (no layout persistence). Phase 50 closes all five gaps with consumer-grade deepening: (1) saved_reports.parent_report_id + DrillDownService + click-through navigation, (2) report_templates platform-curated marketplace + clone service, (3) report_metrics per-landlord composite formulas with safe DSL evaluator (tokenized AST allowlist — Phase 27 SQLi guard pattern), (4) scheduled-report preview endpoint against last-7-days data + auto-refresh polling, (5) landlord_dashboards table + DashboardService::buildPayload + Pages/Dashboards/Show.vue consumer page.

categories.DRILL-DOWN: saved_reports gain nullable parent_report_id FK + drill_field JSON config slot (e.g. drill_field=payment.payment_method → child report filters where payment.payment_method=$segment). DrillDownService::resolveChild(parent SavedReport, $segmentValue) builds the filtered config and runs ReportBuilderService. Builder.vue chart preview wires click → router.visit(route('reports.builder.drill', [parent.id, segment])). GET /reports/builder/{report}/drill route on BuilderController validates ownership + redirects to a builder view with the synthesised child config preloaded.
categories.TEMPLATE-MARKETPLACE: report_templates table (id, slug, name, category enum [finance|occupancy|tenant|maintenance|growth], description, config JSON, is_active, sort_order). ReportTemplate model platform-wide (no TenantScope). Phase50ReportTemplateSeeder seeds 12 curated templates (rent collection rate by building, occupancy heatmap, late-fee revenue, vacancy projection 90d, maintenance cost by category, tenant churn by cohort, etc). ReportTemplateService::cloneFor(User landlord, ReportTemplate) creates a SavedReport scoped to the landlord using template.config. POST /reports/templates/{template}/clone route + GET /reports/templates marketplace page.
categories.CUSTOM-METRICS: report_metrics table (id, landlord_id FK, name, formula text, return_type enum [numeric|percent|currency], is_active, soft-deletes). ReportMetric model with TenantScope. App\Services\Reports\MetricFormulaService::evaluate(ReportMetric, array $row): mixed — tokenizes the formula via a single-pass scanner returning an ordered token stream from a strict allowlist (number literals, ALLOWED_FIELDS identifiers as $row[field] lookups, + - * / operators, parens); evaluates via a Shunting-Yard → RPN pure-PHP evaluator. NEVER eval() / NEVER preg_replace_callback with /e / NEVER assert(). ReportBuilderService gains customMetrics config slot — list of metric IDs, each appended as a computed column to the result rows after the safe SQL runs.
categories.REAL-TIME-PREVIEW: POST /reports/scheduled/preview endpoint accepts {saved_report_id} validates ownership + runs ReportBuilderService against the report config with date filter clamped to last 7 days + returns first 50 rows as JSON (no email, no xlsx, no scheduled_reports row write). Pages/Reports/Scheduled.vue gains a preview panel that polls the endpoint every 30 seconds while open (visibilitychange-aware to pause when tab hidden). Phase50 watchdog asserts ownership check + JSON shape + 50-row cap.
categories.LANDLORD-DASHBOARDS: landlord_dashboards table (id, landlord_id FK, slug VARCHAR(64), name VARCHAR(200), layout JSON, is_default BOOL, soft-deletes; unique on (landlord_id, slug)). LandlordDashboard model with TenantScope + scopeDefault(). layout JSON shape: [{type:metric_card|table|chart, source:{service:Cohort|Noi|Forecast|saved_report, method?, saved_report_id?}, title, span:1-4}]. App\Services\Reports\DashboardService::buildPayload(LandlordDashboard) iterates each widget, resolves the source (calls existing services or runs a saved report through ReportBuilderService), wraps with title + span + an exception-isolated try/catch so a broken widget doesn't poison the dashboard render. Pages/Dashboards/Show.vue consumer page renders the payload + GET /dashboards/{slug} route under role:landlord.
categories.CI: Phase50ReportsDepthSurfaceTest consolidated watchdog asserting all 5 categories' invariants hold. docs/runbooks/reports.md NEW file covering the deepened reports surface (drill-down + marketplace + custom metrics + preview + dashboards) with an SQLi defence retrospective (Phase 27 ALLOWED_* allowlist pattern + Phase 50 MetricFormulaService allowlist pattern). docs/runbooks/alert-thresholds.md gains a sev3 row for report_render_failure_count (a stuck scheduled-report cron is contractual — operator promised landlords weekly KPIs).

findings.title: saved_reports.parent_report_id + drill_field config slot + DrillDownService::resolveChild
findings.evidence: Phase 27 reports are flat — a landlord sees 'total revenue grouped by payment_method' but clicking 'mpesa' does nothing. The drill-down pattern is the dominant consumer-grade reporting feature; without it the report is a dead-end. The infrastructure prerequisites are (a) a parent link so child reports can be navigated to from the chart and (b) a drill_field config that names which dimension the child filters on.
findings.remediation: Migration adds saved_reports.parent_report_id BIGINT UNSIGNED NULL FK references saved_reports(id) onDelete('set null'). Migration adds saved_reports.drill_field VARCHAR(64) nullable (an ALLOWED_FIELDS key like 'payment.payment_method'). App\Services\Reports\DrillDownService::resolveChild(SavedReport $parent, string $segmentValue): array — clones parent.config, appends a filter {field: parent.drill_field, op: '=', value: $segmentValue}, calls ReportBuilderService::run, returns the filtered rows. Validates $parent.drill_field is in ALLOWED_FIELDS to prevent attacker-injected segment fields. SavedReport::children() hasMany self-relation.

findings.title: Builder.vue chart preview wires click-through navigation
findings.evidence: Without UI wire-up, the drill_field is data-only. The Builder.vue preview table renders rows from POST /reports/builder/run; each row representing a unique segment value of drill_field becomes the click-through anchor. Tenant-friendly UX: clicking a row navigates to the drill view with the segment in the URL.
findings.remediation: Builder.vue preview rows gain an @click handler conditional on hasDrillField; calls router.visit(route('reports.builder.drill', {report: savedReport.id, segment: row[drillField]})). When parent.drill_field is null, rows are not clickable (cursor: default). The Vue template references existing props.allowedFields to label which field is the drill-target.

findings.title: GET /reports/builder/{report}/drill route + BuilderController::drill
findings.evidence: Without a route, the drill is a JS-only navigation — and refreshing the page loses state. A real GET endpoint that serves an Inertia page with the synthesised child config preloaded gives bookmarkable + shareable URLs (key Phase-25 API-SURFACE pattern).
findings.remediation: Route::get('/reports/builder/{report}/drill', [BuilderController::class, 'drill'])->name('reports.builder.drill'). Controller method validates $request->user()->id === $report->landlord_id, accepts ?segment= query param, calls DrillDownService::resolveChild, renders Builder.vue with the synthesised child config + parent linkage. The same Builder.vue handles 'new', 'edit', and 'drill' modes via a single mode prop.

findings.title: report_templates table + ReportTemplate model + Phase50ReportTemplateSeeder
findings.evidence: Landlords starting from a blank report builder face a configuration burden — they must know which fields exist, which filters make sense, how to group by building, etc. A platform-curated marketplace of templates (rent collection rate, occupancy heatmap, late-fee revenue, vacancy 90d projection, etc) lets them clone a working report in one click. The data prerequisite is the platform-wide template table.
findings.remediation: Migration create_report_templates_table: id, slug VARCHAR(64) unique, name VARCHAR(200), category ENUM [finance|occupancy|tenant|maintenance|growth|other], description TEXT nullable, config JSON, is_active BOOLEAN default true, sort_order INTEGER default 0, soft-deletes. App\Models\ReportTemplate platform-wide (no TenantScope — these are global). database/seeders/Phase50ReportTemplateSeeder seeds 12 curated rows via updateOrCreate keyed on slug. Migration up() calls the seeder so deployments self-bootstrap.

findings.title: App\Services\Reports\ReportTemplateService::cloneFor(User landlord, ReportTemplate template): SavedReport
findings.evidence: Direct $template->toArray() + SavedReport::create skips audit + landlord_scope validation. A small service enforces the canonical clone path — landlord_id stamped from the User argument (not from the template), config copied by value (not by reference), name suffixed with ' (copy)' to disambiguate.
findings.remediation: App\Services\Reports\ReportTemplateService::cloneFor(User $landlord, ReportTemplate $template, ?string $nameOverride = null): SavedReport — DB::transaction creates a SavedReport with landlord_id=$landlord->id, name=$nameOverride ?? $template->name . ' (copy)', config=deep-copy of $template->config. Phase50 watchdog asserts landlord_id is from the User not the template + config is copied not referenced.

findings.title: GET /reports/templates marketplace + POST /reports/templates/{template}/clone routes
findings.evidence: Without routes the service is callable only programmatically. Landlords need a marketplace page + a one-click clone action.
findings.remediation: Route::get('/reports/templates', [ReportTemplateController::class, 'index'])->name('reports.templates.index') renders Pages/Reports/Templates.vue grouped by category. Route::post('/reports/templates/{template}/clone', [ReportTemplateController::class, 'clone'])->name('reports.templates.clone') calls ReportTemplateService::cloneFor + redirects to /reports/builder?report={new.id}.

findings.title: report_metrics table + ReportMetric model + safe DSL formula contract
findings.evidence: Landlords need composite KPIs that don't exist in the ALLOWED_FIELDS map — e.g. 'occupancy * average_rent' as a single column. SQL-side computation is the wrong place: ReportBuilderService is an allowlist-only engine and adding ad-hoc DB::raw() formulas would bypass the SQLi guard. The correct pattern: compute the row first with safe SQL, then post-process via an in-PHP formula evaluator on a strict allowlist (number literals, ALLOWED_FIELDS identifiers, + - * / parens) — never eval(), never DB::raw() with user input.
findings.remediation: Migration create_report_metrics_table: id, landlord_id FK, name VARCHAR(200), formula TEXT (max 500 chars), return_type ENUM [numeric|percent|currency] default numeric, is_active BOOL, soft-deletes. App\Models\ReportMetric with TenantScope + scope active(). Validation: formula MUST match a strict character set [a-zA-Z0-9._+\-*/() ] — anything else is rejected at validation layer before saving.

findings.title: App\Services\Reports\MetricFormulaService::evaluate — tokenized AST + Shunting-Yard RPN evaluator
findings.evidence: Without a service the formula is dead data. The evaluator MUST be pure-PHP, MUST tokenize via an explicit single-pass scanner, MUST reject any token off the allowlist. Shunting-Yard → RPN handles operator precedence + parens deterministically without recursive parsing complexity. eval()/preg_replace_callback /e/assert() are forbidden — Phase 27 SQLi guard pattern.
findings.remediation: App\Services\Reports\MetricFormulaService::evaluate(ReportMetric $metric, array $row): float|int|null — calls tokenize($formula) returning list<['kind' => number|ident|op|paren_open|paren_close, 'value' => mixed]>; calls shuntingYard($tokens) returning RPN list; calls evalRpn($rpn, $row) which pops tokens onto a stack, idents resolve via $row[$ident] (returns null when missing so formula gracefully degrades), operators consume two operands and push result. Tokenizer rejects any character outside the allowed character class. Phase 27 SQLi golden-query test pattern: pass 20+ malicious payloads (eval-style, comment injection, semicolon termination, /e regex modifier) to evaluate() and assert all reject.

findings.title: ReportBuilderService gains customMetrics config slot — appends computed columns to result rows
findings.evidence: Without integration into the builder execution path, ReportMetric rows are isolated data. The builder needs to know to (a) accept a list of metric IDs in config, (b) resolve them after the safe SQL runs, (c) append each computed value as a new column key on every row.
findings.remediation: ReportBuilderService::run gains a tail: if (!empty($config['custom_metrics'])) { foreach ($config['custom_metrics'] as $metricId) { $metric = ReportMetric::find($metricId); if ($metric === null || $metric->landlord_id !== $landlordId) continue; foreach ($rows as &$row) { $row['_metric_' . $metric->id] = $this->formulaService->evaluate($metric, $row); } } }. Cross-tenant guard inline. Phase50 watchdog asserts a metric belonging to landlord A is silently skipped when run by landlord B.

findings.title: POST /reports/scheduled/preview endpoint runs against last 7 days + returns 50 rows
findings.evidence: Today a landlord configuring a scheduled report saves blind and waits up to a week for the first cron tick to see what gets sent. A preview endpoint that runs the report config against the most recent 7-day window and returns a sample 50 rows gives them immediate feedback.
findings.remediation: Route::post('/reports/scheduled/preview', [ScheduledController::class, 'preview'])->name('reports.scheduled.preview'). Validates saved_report_id is owned by auth landlord. Clones config, injects a date filter clamping date fields to >= now()->subDays(7), clamps limit to 50, calls ReportBuilderService::run. Returns JSON {rows, count, sampled}. Does NOT touch scheduled_reports table — pure preview.

findings.title: Pages/Reports/Scheduled.vue preview panel auto-refresh polling
findings.evidence: Without the Vue wire-up, the preview endpoint is service-only. The polling pattern (Phase 26 PWA-DEPTH-2 useConnection-aware): refresh every 30s while the panel is visible; pause when document.hidden (background tab). Network polite + caretaker-laptop-friendly.
findings.remediation: Pages/Reports/Scheduled.vue gains a preview-panel section with a fetch loop driven by setInterval(30000); document.addEventListener('visibilitychange', ...) pauses + resumes; manual refresh button + last-fetched timestamp + row count badge. Phase50 watchdog source-greps the file for 'preview' + 'visibilitychange' to prove the wire-up exists.

findings.title: Phase50 ownership test on /reports/scheduled/preview
findings.evidence: A preview endpoint that accepts saved_report_id but doesn't verify ownership leaks one landlord's report data to another. Phase-27 surface already enforces the pattern; the watchdog records it explicitly.
findings.remediation: Phase50 test: landlord A creates SavedReport; landlord B POSTs /reports/scheduled/preview with A's report id; assert 403 or 404; assert no rows returned.

findings.title: landlord_dashboards table + LandlordDashboard model with TenantScope
findings.evidence: Phase 36 [INSIGHT] Dashboard.vue widgets are ad-hoc — there's no table to persist which widgets a landlord prefers, in what layout, with what defaults. Without persistent layouts, every landlord sees the same hardcoded composition. The data prerequisite is the dashboard layout table.
findings.remediation: Migration create_landlord_dashboards_table: id, landlord_id FK, slug VARCHAR(64), name VARCHAR(200), layout JSON, is_default BOOLEAN default false, soft-deletes, timestamps. Unique on (landlord_id, slug). App\Models\LandlordDashboard with TenantScope + scopeDefault() + scopeForSlug(). layout JSON shape: list<['type' => 'metric_card'|'table'|'chart', 'source' => ['service' => 'Cohort'|'Noi'|'Forecast'|'saved_report', 'method' => string|null, 'saved_report_id' => int|null, 'args' => array|null], 'title' => string, 'span' => 1|2|3|4]>.

findings.title: App\Services\Reports\DashboardService::buildPayload + Pages/Dashboards/Show.vue consumer
findings.evidence: Without a resolver service, every Vue page reading the layout would have to duplicate the source-resolution + exception-isolation logic. A single service that produces a render-ready payload keeps the consumer page thin + the service unit-testable.
findings.remediation: App\Services\Reports\DashboardService::buildPayload(LandlordDashboard $dashboard): array — iterates layout, resolves each widget source (CohortService/NoiService/ForecastService/ReportBuilderService::run for saved_report kind), wraps with title + span + a try/catch returning {error: $e->getMessage()} for broken widgets (one broken widget MUST NOT poison the dashboard). Pages/Dashboards/Show.vue renders the payload in a 4-column responsive grid using span values.

findings.title: GET /dashboards/{slug} route under role:landlord
findings.evidence: Without a route, the dashboard surface is service-only. Landlords need a per-slug URL to bookmark + share with team members. Authorization gate is role:landlord — caretakers + tenants don't get the operator-grade dashboard.
findings.remediation: Route::get('/dashboards/{slug}', [DashboardController::class, 'show'])->middleware('role:landlord')->name('dashboards.show'). Controller resolves LandlordDashboard::forSlug($slug)->first() with TenantScope active (so only the auth landlord's row matches); 404 if not found; renders Pages/Dashboards/Show.vue with DashboardService::buildPayload.

findings.title: Phase50ReportsDepthSurfaceTest consolidated watchdog
findings.evidence: Phase 50 changes span 5 services + 5 migrations + 4 models + 3 Vue components + 1 seeder — drift is easy without a single watchdog. The Phase 37-49 pattern (one *SurfaceTest per cycle) consolidates the invariants.
findings.remediation: tests/Feature/Reports/Phase50ReportsDepthSurfaceTest with 18+ tests across the 5 categories: schema columns, service contracts, formula evaluator allowlist rejection of 10+ malicious payloads, ownership enforcement on preview endpoint, landlord_dashboards TenantScope works, DashboardService exception-isolation, template seeder populates rows, ReportTemplateService::cloneFor stamps landlord_id from User.

findings.title: docs/runbooks/reports.md new file covering the deepened surface
findings.evidence: The reports surface is now landlord-facing operator-critical: cron deliveries + dashboards + custom formulas. A dedicated runbook is the right home for the operator playbook (debug stuck schedule, replay a missed delivery, audit a custom formula for safety, etc).
findings.remediation: docs/runbooks/reports.md sections: (1) Phase-27 MVP recap + ALLOWED_FIELDS allowlist; (2) Phase-50 drill-down hierarchy + DrillDownService cascade; (3) Template marketplace + adding a curated template; (4) Custom metrics + the safe DSL evaluator + SQLi guard retrospective; (5) Scheduled-report preview + polling cadence; (6) Landlord dashboards + widget source resolution; (7) Incident playbook — stuck cron / broken widget / failed formula evaluation.

findings.title: docs/runbooks/alert-thresholds.md gains report_render_failure_count sev3 row
findings.evidence: Scheduled reports are a landlord-facing contractual deliverable (weekly KPIs etc). A SendScheduledReports cron tick that throws on row N silently logs + continues — without an aggregate failure gauge, on-call doesn't know a landlord stopped receiving their reports.
findings.remediation: alert-thresholds.md gains: report_render_failure_count sev3 threshold 3/24h (more than 3 failed scheduled-report renders in a day signals systemic problem worth paging). Gauge wiring deferred to a follow-up cycle (threshold row defines the contract).
